name: Valencia
tagline: Mediterranean Living at Half the Price of Barcelona
description: Valencia offers everything expats love about coastal Spain — paella on the beach, world-class architecture at the City of Arts & Sciences, and a genuine Mediterranean lifestyle — but at a fraction of Barcelona or Madrid prices. Spain's third-largest city combines historic charm with modern innovation, making it one of Europe's best-kept FIRE secrets.

quickFacts.visa: Non-Lucrative Visa (must show sufficient funds, no work allowed). Beckham Law may apply for new tax residents. Path to permanent residency and citizenship after 10 years.
quickFacts.safetyRating: Very Safe — one of Spain's safest large cities, standard urban precautions apply
quickFacts.nearestAirport: Valencia Airport (VLC) — 15 min from city center
quickFacts.climate: Mediterranean with mild winters (10-16°C) and hot summers (25-33°C). Over 300 days of sunshine per year.

housing.description: Bright 1-bedroom apartment in Ruzafa or Benimaclet, walkable bohemian neighborhoods with local character

dining.description: Menu del día lunches for 10-12 euros, tapas evenings, horchata and fartons at a neighborhood horchatería

healthcare.description: Public healthcare enrollment plus basic private insurance for faster specialist access

transportation.description: Valenbisi bike-share membership and monthly metro/bus pass for citywide coverage

entertainment.description: Beach days at Malvarrosa, free Turia park cycling, local fallas celebrations, museum visits

1M.lifestyle: A bright 1-bedroom in Ruzafa or Benimaclet, both walkable neighborhoods with local character. Menu del dia lunches and tapas evenings keep dining costs low. Getting around by Valenbisi bike-share and metro. Public healthcare with a basic private supplement. Valencia is one of Spain's most affordable major cities, so this budget covers a comfortable baseline with room for regular cheap meals out and beach days at Malvarrosa.

housing.description: Spacious 2-bedroom apartment in Eixample or El Carmen with balcony and renovated interiors

dining.description: Regular dining at Ruzafa's acclaimed restaurants, weekend paella at beachside chiringuitos, wine bar evenings

healthcare.description: Comprehensive private insurance covering Hospital La Fe and top clinics, dental included

transportation.description: Monthly transit pass, Valenbisi, occasional taxi rides, weekend car rental for Albufera trips

entertainment.description: Oceanogràfic visits, sailing lessons at the marina, La Liga matches at Mestalla, weekend trips to Albufera

2M.lifestyle: A spacious 2-bedroom in Eixample or El Carmen with a balcony. Dining out at good restaurants several times a week, with weekend paella at beachside spots. Comprehensive private health insurance and a car rental on weekends for trips to the Albufera wetlands or nearby coast. Valencia CF matches at Mestalla and sailing lessons at the marina. Valencia's affordability means this budget delivers a genuinely comfortable life with little day-to-day worry about costs.

housing.description: Elegant 3-bedroom apartment in Gran Via or a renovated loft near the Central Market with terrace views

dining.description: Fine dining at Ricard Camarena and Riff, private paella masterclasses, curated Valencian wine experiences

healthcare.description: Premium private insurance with international coverage, executive health screenings, wellness programs

transportation.description: Leased car for countryside excursions, metro pass, regular taxi rides across the city

entertainment.description: Marina sailing club membership, VIP Fallas festival experiences, concert season tickets at Palau de les Arts

3M.lifestyle: An elegant apartment near the Central Market or Gran Via with a terrace and views. Regular dining at top restaurants including places like Ricard Camarena, a leased car for countryside excursions, and premium private healthcare. Sailing club membership at the marina and season tickets at Palau de les Arts. Valencia is affordable enough that this budget delivers a genuinely affluent life with regular travel and no real constraints on daily spending.

housing.description: Luxury penthouse near the City of Arts & Sciences or a restored historic mansion in El Carmen with rooftop terrace

dining.description: Private chef preparing Valencian and international cuisine, regular Michelin dining, curated wine cellar management

healthcare.description: International VIP health coverage, personal physician, longevity and wellness programs at top clinics

transportation.description: Luxury vehicle lease, occasional private driver, weekend drives to the Costa Blanca

entertainment.description: Private yacht moored at La Marina, art collecting in Ruzafa galleries, VIP season box at Mestalla, exclusive club memberships

domesticHelp.description: Full-time housekeeper, weekly deep cleaning, personal assistant for daily logistics

luxuryExtras.description: Personal trainer, private Spanish tutors, bespoke tailoring, premium coworking space, yacht marina fees

5M.lifestyle: A luxury penthouse near the City of Arts and Sciences or a restored historic home in El Carmen with a rooftop terrace. Regular dining at the city's best restaurants, a housekeeper several times a week, and business class for longer flights. A luxury car for coastal drives and premium private healthcare. Valencia's low costs mean this budget goes very far -- daily life is upper-affluent with significant room for travel and entertainment.

housing.description: Grand historic estate in the old town or ultra-modern waterfront penthouse with panoramic views from port to mountains

dining.description: Full-time private chef team, rare wine acquisitions, legendary hosted dinner parties, guest chef residencies from Madrid and Barcelona

healthcare.description: Global concierge medicine, dedicated medical team, fly-anywhere coverage, biohacking and longevity protocols

transportation.description: Chauffeured luxury fleet, private jet access for European getaways, superyacht berthing at La Marina

entertainment.description: Art patronage and gallery curation, private island retreats in the Balearics, cultural festival sponsorship, VIP global experiences

domesticHelp.description: Full household staff: butler, chef team, housekeepers, personal assistant, property manager, gardener

luxuryExtras.description: Costa Blanca vacation villa management, charitable foundation, bespoke Mediterranean yacht itineraries, art collection curation, personal brand consulting

10M.lifestyle: A grand historic property in the old town or an ultra-modern waterfront penthouse with panoramic views. A cook several times a week, a full-time housekeeper, and full private healthcare. Business class as default, first class for longer trips. Regular fine dining and a nice car with driver when needed. Valencia is one of the most affordable cities on this list, so this budget exceeds what the city can realistically absorb -- you will likely find it hard to spend this much here without a second property.
